close_reason: Root cause: CFBundleDisplayName unset, so the SDK sent device-select-req with empty ciqApp and GCM dropped it. Fixed in Support/Info.plist; multi-device status tracking also fixed in WatchRemoteService. Round-trip verified in device syslog.
description: Watch app (RaceCompass-fr245.prg) is sideloaded and installed on the FR245 Music. In the iOS app, Settings > Connect Garmin Watch opens Garmin Connect Mobile but the device-picker sheet never appears, so WatchRemoteService never gets the callback and no data flows. iOS SDK is official garmin/connectiq-companion-app-sdk-ios 1.8.0; Support/Info.plist URL schemes (racecompass-ciq, gcm-ciq) verified present in source. Next step: build to the iPhone 16 Pro (kriphone — the phone actually paired to the watch; the 12 Pro is NOT) via Xcode, tap Connect Garmin Watch, and read the ConnectIQ SDK console output to see what GCM is rejecting. Also verify the installed app's built Info.plist actually contains CFBundleURLTypes (GENERATE_INFOPLIST_FILE=YES + INFOPLIST_FILE merge), and that GCM on that phone is up to date with the FR245M as its active device.
title: Garmin watch pairing: GCM opens but no device-selection sheet appears
